title: Container Image Vulnerability Scan Failures
dataSources: Scan exit code, `Status: ERROR` in SARIF
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="container:scan"
| where scan_status!="SUCCESS" OR match(_raw,"(?i)(timeout|failed to pull|manifest unknown)")
| stats count by image_ref, scanner, error_message
| sort -count
description: CI gate failures when Trivy/Grype/ECR scanning cannot pull image or scanner errors‚Äîdistinct from found CVEs (UC-12.3.2).
value: CI gate failures when Trivy/Grype/ECR scanning cannot pull image or scanner errors‚Äîdistinct from found CVEs (UC-12.3.2).
implementation: Alert on scanner infrastructure failures separately from policy violations. Retry with backoff; page platform team on registry outages.
visualization: Table (failed scans), Line chart (failure rate), Single value (open scan errors).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Trivy JSON output, Harbor webhook.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Scan exit code, `Status: ERROR` in SARIF.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Alert on scanner infrastructure failures separately from policy violations. Retry with backoff; page platform team on registry outages.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="container:scan"
| where scan_status!="SUCCESS" OR match(_raw,"(?i)(timeout|failed to pull|manifest unknown)")
| stats count by image_ref, scanner, error_message
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Container Image Vulnerability Scan Failures** ‚Äî CI gate failures when Trivy/Grype/ECR scanning cannot pull image or scanner errors‚Äîdistinct from found CVEs (UC-12.3.2).

Documented **Data sources**: Scan exit code, `Status: ERROR` in SARIF. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Trivy JSON output, Harbor webhook. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: container:scan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="container:scan". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where scan_status!="SUCCESS" OR match(_raw,"(?i)(timeout|failed to pull|manifest unknown)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by image_ref, scanner, error_message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the repository admin UI and download logs. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed scans), Line chart (failure rate), Single value (open scan errors).
grandmaExplanation: We watch this part of your delivery chain for risks and reliability so issues show up before they hit customers.
knownFalsePositives: Alerts from scheduled scans, pilot repositories, or vendor API throttling during bulk operations.
